name: Zerodha Cycle & Duplicate Detector
scenario: Detect circular references in Zerodha's payment routing graph using Floyd's algorithm.

core.description: hasCycle(head): Floyd's O(n) O(1). Test: cycle→true, no cycle→false.
detectCycleStart(head): Phase 2 reset. Test: return entry node or null.
cycleLength(head): count from entry. Test: correct cycle length.
findDuplicate(int[] nums): array as implicit LL + Floyd's. O(n) O(1). Test:[1,3,4,2,2]→2.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): cycle true, entry correct, length correct, duplicate=2.
README: explains Phase 2 proof — why reset slow finds cycle entry.

bonus.description: getIntersectionNode(headA,headB): O(n+m) O(1) two-pointer. Test: shared value.
Happy Number (LeetCode #202): cycle detection on number sequence. Test: 19→true, 2→false.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. hasCycle: fast+slow start at head. Loop: fast=fast.next.next, slow=slow.next. If slow==fast→cycle. If fast==null or fast.next==null→no cycle. detectCycleStart Phase 2: after Phase 1 meeting, reset slow=head. Both advance at speed 1. When slow==fast: that's the cycle entry. cycleLength: from entry, advance and count until returning. findDuplicate: nums[i] is the 'next' pointer. Two nodes with same value mean two nodes point to the same next → cycle. Phase 1: detect. Phase 2: find entry = duplicate.
